rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNIE ACTKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/31/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) There is no such filing as a response to Examiner Notes. A response to examiner notes must be filed as an amended pleading, an amendment to a pleading, or a supplement. (See California Rule of Court 7.3.) 2) A request for fees by court appointed attorney Dennis G. Mesina attached to a response to examiner notes is not proper procedure. Dennis Mesina may petition ex parte for fees in accordance with LRSF 14.11 and LRSF 14.52 or an amendment to the petition must be filed with notice and Dennis Mesina joining in the petition. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/16/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JENNIE ACTKINSON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/31/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) There is no such filing as a response to Examiner Notes. A response to examiner notes must be filed as an amended pleading, an amendment to a pleading, or a supplement. (See California Rule of Court 7.3.) 2) Any increase in the request for reimbursement from the original petition is a material change and requires an amendment to the petition and notice. (See California Rule of Court 7.53). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/16/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Determination Of Heirship
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of diligence and progress. The following issues raised by the tentative ruling for the initial hearing on 11/02/2021 (and also e-mailed to counsel on 11/22/2021) have not been addressed: 1) No courtesy copy of the (amended) petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received (or lodged). 3) No proof of service of notice is on file. 4) The name and date of death of the decedent's spouse must be provided. 5) The names and dates of death of the decedent's parents must be provided. 6) The name and address of a personal representative of each post-deceased heir's estate must be provided, together with a copy of court-appointment documentation. If no personal representative has been appointed, the names and addresses of all successors in interest under the laws of each post-deceased heir's domicile must be provided. If the petitioner and administrator anticipate that such successors in interest will proceed under Probate Code section 11802(c), their affidavits or declarations under section 13101 must be on file. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The first account covering a 4-year period from 01/16/2012 through 01/15/2016 reported no financial transactions. The administrator alleges that he has been unable to obtain copies of statements or other records of the decedent's bank accounts. The balance of each bank account listed on the inventory and appraisal is the amount the administrator collected from Chase and State Controller in 2019 after letters were issued, and may not be the date-of-death balance. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VIRGINIA RAMOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/24/2022. Third continuance same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) On petition page 2 lines 5-6 the petition alleges Letters Testamentary were issued when the petition alleges decedent died intestate and the court issued Letters of administration. 2) The property on hand at beginning of account must match the inventory and appraisal values. It appears the account must be amended to conform to Probate Code 1061 et seq. 3) Liabilities should be on a separate schedule in accordance with Probate Code 1063(g) not as part of the assets on hand at the beginning of the account. 4) On Schedule C - Disbursements please explain and file a receipt for entry 10/12/2021 Accounting Data for $1,200.00. 5) On Schedule C - Disbursements please explain the entries under Miscellaneous and file receipts for the following: a. 10/21/2019 for $47.08 b. 10/21/2019 for $10.00 c. 10/30/2018 for $1,173.63 d. 11/6/2018 for $59.83 e. 11/15/2018 for $176.82 f. 11/6/2018 for $80.16 g. 11/7/2018 for $32.14 8) On schedule C - Disbursements please explain entry 9/19/20219 for "Trust Record" for $300.00 and file a receipt. 6) On schedule C - Disbursements please file a receipts for the following entries: a. 8/16/2019 San Francisco Public Utilities Commission for $7,575.42 b. 8/24/2020 San Francisco Water for $4991.51 7) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (See LRSF 14.34 D.) An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/9/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JEWEL DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/24/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/9/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And Termination Of Trust Pursuant To Probate Code Sections 15403 And 15409
rulings.Rulings: Continued to first available date 05/24/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Required courtesy copy received late. continued to allow examiner time to complete review. Unclear if venue is proper. Please file a copy of the 16061.7 notification and any other information to explain why San Francisco is the proper venue. Note: Petition hasnt been reviewed but petitions to approve settlement must include the following (1) a citation to the Probate Code section under which the petition is presented; (2) the legal standard governing the petition; (3) a summary of the key terms of the settlement agreement; and (4) a statement regarding the parties' understanding of what they believe court approval means.
